learning_outcomes.description: Illustrate the geoscientific processes in the formation of mineral deposits.
Communicate reserve and resource estimates according to the JORC standard.
Examine the error and risk in the orebody characterisation by applying the principles of sampling and compositing across drill samples
Apply traditional resource estimation techniques to the development of a resource estimate.
Collaborate as a team to develop a resource estimate using geostatistics with industry-standard software.
Defend the recommendation of further drilling locations that enhance confidence in orebody characterisation.

assessments.task_description:
Take home activity set (individual) containing numerous short answer and calculation questions covering the Geoscience content (the first 5-6 weeks). This will be released 2 weeks prior to the due date.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

Please refer to Blackboard for a detailed marking criteria.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please submit word/pdf document addressing each question via turn-it-in.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Estimation of a coal resource in keeping with JORC parameters. A detailed task sheet for this assignment will be issued at release.

Students will partake in a brief (5-10 min) individual online interview where they need to provide a 'client update' (executive summary style slide deck with Q&A) to demonstrate their understanding of the assessment independent of AI. This will be organised at a mutually suitable time shortly after submission.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

Please refer to Blackboard for a detailed marking criteria.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Estimation of a mineral (metalliferous) resource in keeping with JORC parameters. A detailed task sheet for this assignment will be issued upon release.

This item will be peer reviewed upon completion. If, for whatever reason, you find that your group is not functioning effectively, please contact your Course Coordinator for support.

This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.

Please refer to Blackboard for a detailed marking criteria.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Please submit a single group report via turn-it-in.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
